title: USDA Common Metadata Production Contract
format_neutral_rules: contracts/document-model-productions/common-metadata.contract.json#laws — field categories per spec kind, spec_kind_field_admission matrix, listOp subfield semantics, repeated-field semantics, and extension-field rule are pinned in the format-neutral production. This contract adds USDA-specific grammar dispatch: metadata_contexts, metadata_key_classification, and the keyword production list per context.

included: last-authored value wins for repeated non-listOp metadata fields
last-authored subfield wins for repeated listOp metadata subfields
storage of metadata on layer, prim, attribute, relationship, and variant specs

storage_mapping.generic_key_value: store under the authored field token using the value parser result
storage_mapping.doc: store as the document-model documentation field
storage_mapping.standalone_doc_string: store a valid standalone layer doc string as the document-model documentation field
storage_mapping.known_core_field: store under the matching document-model core field token only when the spelling is valid for the current metadata production context
storage_mapping.listOp_metadata: store as an authored listOp value of the element type implied by the field

metadata_key_classification.rule: Classify the authored metadata spelling exactly once at the parser boundary with the current metadata context before selecting field storage or value typing.
metadata_key_classification.generic_identifier_fields: KeyValueMetadata and ListOpMetadata use Identifier, which is a KeywordlessIdentifier. Reserved USDA keywords are not legal generic metadata keys.
metadata_key_classification.known_core_fields: Authored spellings matching document-model core field names resolve to core field tokens only if they are accepted by the grammar production for the current spec form. A core field token is not evidence that the spelling is legal in every metadata block.
metadata_key_classification.extension_fields: Only spellings that are neither aliases, known context-specific productions, nor reserved USDA keywords may use document-model extension field-token lookup.

laws: common metadata parsing must be available to all spec forms that accept SharedMetadata
standalone layer doc strings inside the layer metadata block must parse as documentation metadata rather than requiring a key-value metadata identifier
metadata keyword productions must be context-sensitive; a keyword accepted in prim metadata must not be accepted as generic KeyValueMetadata on attributes or relationships
metadata parser implementations must carry the full metadata context through dispatch; collapsing all non-layer specs into one context is non-conformant
variant statement metadata uses the same production family as prim metadata and must not be treated as generic non-layer metadata
reserved USDA keywords must not be routed through extension-field token lookup when the current metadata context has no matching keyword production
metadata value typing for known fields must be selected from the classified metadata key rather than by repeated string comparisons
metadata parsing must not drop composition-related fields merely because composition semantics are deferred
JSON materialization is allowed only for validation adapters and diagnostics

alias spellings (doc→documentation, inherits→inheritPaths, variantSets→variantSetNames, variants→variantSelection, relocates→layerRelocates) are USDA grammar keywords resolved to their canonical document-model field tokens at the parser boundary, never through extension-field token lookup
format_neutral_laws_hoisted: metadata field-name identity is via token, not raw string keys (document-model-productions/common-metadata.contract.json#laws)

each spec kind admits only the union of shared and kind-specific fields; emitting a non-admitted field on a spec kind is a violation (document-model-productions/common-metadata.contract.json#spec_kind_field_admission)
listOp metadata uses {explicit, prepend, append, delete, reorder} subfields stored independently (document-model-productions/common-metadata.contract.json#listop_subfields)
a single item authored where a listOp is allowed becomes an explicit listOp (document-model-productions/common-metadata.contract.json#listop_subfields)
None authored where a listOp is allowed becomes an empty listOp on the named subfield (document-model-productions/common-metadata.contract.json#listop_subfields)

within a single source layer, repeated non-listOp metadata fields use last-one-wins; repeated listOp subfields use last-one-wins per subfield (document-model-productions/common-metadata.contract.json#repeated_field_semantics)
golden_groups: generic metadata on each spec kind
